zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Reed - MA於大約1615年4月23日出生在英國時，她的父親William Reede Sr.28歲，母親Avis Chapman-2歲。她於1640年與Thomas Dyer - MA在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1667年12月4日在美國麻薩諸塞州諾福爾可維姆斯享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Reed - MA于大约1615年4月23日出生在英国时，她的父亲William Reede Sr.28岁，母亲Avis Chapman-2岁。她于1640年与Thomas Dyer - MA在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可維姆斯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1667年12月4日在美国马萨诸塞州諾福爾可維姆斯享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Reed
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English and Older Scots red(e) ‘red’, no doubt denoting someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion.

English: from Middle English ride, rede, rude (Old English rīed, rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’. The surname may be topographic for someone who lived in or near a clearing, or habitational, for someone who lived at one of a number of places so named, including Rede Court in Strood (Kent), Rides in Eastchurch (Kent), Ride Way in Ewhurst (Surrey), and Reed Farm in Wadhurst (Sussex). The word is particularly common in the southeastern counties of England, from Kent to the Isle of Wight. See also Rider and Reader .

English: habitational name from Read (Lancashire), Reed (Hertfordshire), or Rede (Suffolk). The Lancashire placename derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe, female roe deer’ + hēafod ‘head’. The Hertfordshire placename derives from Old English rȳhth ‘rough piece of ground’. The etymology of the Suffolk placename is uncertain.

variants.name: Read
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Reed  .

History: A family called Read were established in America in the early 18th century by John Read, who was born in Dublin, sixth in descent from Sir Thomas Read of Berkshire, England. His son, George Read (1733–98), was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence, and as a lawyer helped frame the Constitution.

variants.name: Ried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Friedrich, Johann.
German: topographic name from Middle High German riet ‘damp, mossy area, wet land’ or ‘reed grass, swamp’.
German: habitational name from any of various places so named in Hesse, Baden-Württemberg, Austria, Switzerland.

variants.name: Reede
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 423, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Reeds
meanings: English: variant of Reed , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Reetz   or   Rietz  .

variants.name: Redd
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  .

variants.name: Reid
meanings: Scottish: variant of Reed , of which Reid is the common modern Scots spelling.

History: Samuel Chester Reid (1783–1861), the sea captain who designed the stars and stripes form of the present the American flag, was born at Norwich, CT. His father, Lt. John Reid, a former British naval officer of a distinguished Glasgow family, had resigned his commission and joined the cause of the American Revolution.

variants.name: Roy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Normand, Andre, Jacques, Marcel, Emile, Laurent, Pierre, Cecile, Donat, Gilles.  Indian Dipak, Sumit, Amit, Ajit, Dipankar, Partha, Ranjan, Shyamal, Aloke, Amitava, Chitra, Gautam.
Scottish: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Gaelic ruadh ‘red’. Compare Reed .
English (of Norman origin): from Old French roi ‘king’, used as a nickname (see 3 below) and also as a personal name. Compare Ray 2.

variants.name: Rider
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English ridere ‘horseman’, perhaps with a specific application such as ‘forest ranger’, ‘horseman who guards crops’, or ‘mounted warrior, knight’. Compare Knight .
English: habitational name from Ryther, near Selby (Yorkshire), probably from Old English ryther ‘clearing’.

English: topographic name from Middle English ridere ‘dweller at a clearing’ (see Reeder ) or ‘dweller by a small stream’, depending on which sense of Middle English ride the surname is derived from. These topographic senses of Rider are limited to the southern counties of England (especially Surrey, Sussex, and Hampshire), where topographic names in -er and the Middle English words ride ‘clearing’ and rithe ‘small stream’ are mainly found. See also Reed .

nameMeanings.name: MA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Li, Hong, Hung, Wing, Wai, Wei, Ying, Ming, Ping, Chi, Cuong, Dong, Jin, Lan, Minh, Thanh, Lien, Trung, Binh, Hao, Phat, Quan, Quang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Phong, Sha, Tam, Tian, Cho, Chong, Chul, Dae Yong, Eun Hee, In Sook.  Vietnamese Cuong, Dong, Minh, Thanh, Lien, Trung, Binh, Phat, Quan, Quang.

Chinese: Mandarin or Cantonese form of the surname 馬, which means ‘horse’: (i) from the first element of Mafu (馬服), the name of a fief (located in Handan in Hebei province) or an honorific title that was granted to Zhao She, a prince of the state of Zhao during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). The name Mafu (馬服) was later acquired as a surname and shortened to Ma (馬). (ii) from the adopted name of Xi-Li Ji'en (習禮吉恩), an official in the Jin dynasty (1115–1234  AD ), who changed his original name to Ma Qingxiang (馬慶祥) when he moved from Central Asia to Lintao (in present-day Gansu province). (iii) from the adopted name of Yue Naihe (月乃和), a commander-in-chief in the late Jin dynasty (1115–1234  AD ), who changed his original name to Ma Zu Chang (馬祖常). (iv) this surname is also borne by Muslims of the Hui ethnic group, located mainly in northwestern China.

Chinese: Mandarin or Cantonese form of the surname 麻, which denotes a herbaceous plant such as hemp or flax: (i) from the placename Ma (麻), the name of a fief (located in Macheng in Hubei province) that was granted to an official of the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the personal name of Ma Ying (麻婴), an official in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

variants.name: Mah
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Suey, Foon, Ke, Mei, Meng, Sun, Tsz, Wah, Chai, Chee, Cheng.
Chinese and Korean: variant of   Ma  .

variants.name: Mahr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Franz, Gerhard, Hans, Helmut, Ingo, Klaus, Reinhold.
German: topographic name from Middle Low German mer, mar ‘lake, marsh; draining ditch’.
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(e), Middle Low German mār(e) ‘ghost, night mare’.

variants.name: Moua
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chao, Ying, Mee, Tou, Yia, Chue, Kou, Nhia, Tong, Yang, Yee, Chia, Pao, Vang, Neng, Blia, Chong, Koua, Yer, Houa, Shoua, Thai, Blong, Soua, Mai, Long, Thao, Dang, Thong, Bao, Pha, Be, Hue, Ly, Mang, Nang.
Southeast Asian (Hmong): from the name of the Moua clan of the Hmong people in Laos, China, and Vietnam; in Chinese characters it is written 馬 (see   Ma   1). Compare   Mua   1.

variants.name: Maa
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 馬, see   Ma   1.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Reed - MA was born about 23 April 1615, in England, her father, William Reede Sr., was 28 and her mother, Avis Chapman, was -2. She married Thomas Dyer - MA in 1640, in Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 4 December 1667, in Weymouth, Norfolk, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 52.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Reed - MA(이)가 대략 1615년 4월 23일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Reede Sr.(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avis Chapman(은)는 -2세였다. 그녀는 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 베이모우스에서 Thomas Dyer - MA(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1667년 12월 4일 미국 매사추세츠 주 노르폴크 베이모우스에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Reed - MA nasceu aproximadamente 23 de abril de 1615, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Reede Sr., tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Avis Chapman, tinha -2 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Dyer - MA em 1640, em Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1667, em Weymouth, Norfolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Reed - MA родилась около 23 апреля 1615 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Reede Sr. было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Avis Chapman, -2. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Dyer - MA в 1640 г. в местности вимоут, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 4 декабря 1667 г. в местности вимоут, норфолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Reed - MA nació aproximadamente 23 de abril de 1615, en Inglaterra, su papá, William Reede Sr., tenía 28 años y su mamá, Avis Chapman, tenía -2 años. Se casó con Thomas Dyer - MA en 1640, en Weymouth, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1667, en Weymouth, Norfolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 52 años.
